stories.title: Judge Walks Off Rays — Ends 11-Game Homer Drought, Yankees Win 2-0 (Tier 1)
stories.posting_window: 8:00 AM ET (walk-off recap), 10:00 AM ET (AL East race angle)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Aaron Judge. 11 games without a homer.

First pitch of the 9th inning off Kevin Kelly.

Two-run walk-off. Yankees 2, Rays 0.

That's HR No. 17 — and the AL East just got a little tighter. ⚾

#BallparkBanter #Yankees #MLB
x_posts.posting_time: 8:00 AM ET (walk-off recap), 10:00 AM ET (AL East race angle)

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
After Judge's walk-off Sunday:

Yankees: 31-22
Rays lead: 4.5 games

A week ago it was 5.5. Ryan Weathers gave NY 7 scoreless innings, then Judge did the rest.

AL East is a race again. ⚾

#BallparkBanter #Yankees #MLB
x_posts.posting_time: 8:00 AM ET (walk-off recap), 10:00 AM ET (AL East race angle)

facebook.long_form:
Aaron Judge had gone 11 games without a home run. He was 1-for-24 over the stretch. The narrative was building: was something wrong with baseball's most dangerous slugger?

Then came Sunday night.

Ryan Weathers pitched seven scoreless innings to keep the Yankees in a 0-0 game against the first-place Rays. Judge came to the plate in the ninth. On the first pitch — a sinker from Kevin Kelly — Judge turned on it and drove a 2-run, walk-off home run to right-center field. Final score: Yankees 2, Rays 0. Slump over.

HR No. 17. His 4th career walk-off homer. And with the Yankees now at 31-22, the Rays' AL East lead is down to 4.5 games. A week ago it was 5.5.

Judge has a way of doing this — going quiet long enough to let you forget what he's capable of, then reminding you in the most theatrical way possible.

Is the Yankees-Rays race heating up? It might be.

---
facebook.image_caption: Judge. Walk-off. HR No. 17. The 11-game drought is over — and the AL East race just got tighter. ⚾
facebook.posting_time: 8:00 AM ET (walk-off recap), 10:00 AM ET (AL East race angle)

stories.title: Corbin Carroll One Triple From D-Backs Franchise Record (Tier 1)
stories.posting_window: 10:30 AM ET (record chase), 12:00 PM ET (pace analysis)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Corbin Carroll went 4-for-4 with TWO triples Sunday.

He now has 51 career triples. The D-Backs franchise record: 52 (Stephen Drew).

One triple from history.

He got there in 537 games. Drew needed 773.

Carroll is a different kind of player. ⚾

#BallparkBanter #Dbacks #MLB
x_posts.posting_time: 10:30 AM ET (record chase), 12:00 PM ET (pace analysis)

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Corbin Carroll leads MLB with 8 triples in 2026.

Among active players in career triples, only Mike Trout and Starling Marte have more.

He is 26 years old.

The art of the triple is not dead. ⚾

#BallparkBanter #Dbacks #BaseballStats
x_posts.posting_time: 10:30 AM ET (record chase), 12:00 PM ET (pace analysis)

facebook.long_form:
Ask a casual baseball fan what the most exciting play in the sport is and they'll say a home run. Ask a purist. They'll say a triple.

Corbin Carroll has been rewriting the history of the three-bagger all season — and now he's one triple away from franchise immortality.

On Sunday, Carroll went 4-for-4 with two triples against Colorado, pushing his career total to 51. Stephen Drew holds the Arizona Diamondbacks franchise record at 52. One triple. That's it.

What makes this chase even more remarkable is the pace. Carroll has played 537 career games to reach 51 triples. Drew needed 773 games to get to 52. Carroll is getting there in fewer at-bats, at a younger age (26), with a legitimate shot at blowing past the record entirely.

He also leads MLB in 2026 with 8 triples, and trails only Mike Trout and Starling Marte among active players in career total triples.

The home run era has squeezed out the triple. Carroll doesn't care.

Watch for the record to fall this week.

---
facebook.image_caption: Corbin Carroll is one triple from the D-Backs franchise record. He got there in 537 games — Stephen Drew needed 773. ⚾
facebook.posting_time: 10:30 AM ET (record chase), 12:00 PM ET (pace analysis)

stories.title: Hernandez Walk-Off Grand Slam in 0-0 Game — Marlins Sweep Mets (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
A 0-0 game. Ninth inning. Pinch-hitter up.

Heriberto Hernandez hits a 416-foot walk-off grand slam off Devin Williams.

Marlins win 4-0. Mets swept.

First walk-off slam in a 0-0 game since 2013. Baseball is incredible. ⚾

#BallparkBanter #Marlins #MLB

facebook.long_form:
This is one for the baseball history books.

On Sunday in Miami, the Marlins and Mets played into the ninth inning with the score locked at 0-0. Both starters had been excellent. Neither team had scored. Then Heriberto Hernandez — coming off the bench as a pinch-hitter — stepped into the box with the bases loaded and absolutely crushed a 416-foot grand slam off Devin Williams.

Walk-off. Marlins win 4-0. Three-game sweep.

Here's the wild stat that puts this in context: it was the first walk-off grand slam in a 0-0 game since Justin Maxwell hit one for the Royals on September 22, 2013. Thirteen years. Not one time in 13 years had someone done what Hernandez did on Sunday.

The Marlins needed it, too. They had dropped three of four to Atlanta earlier in the week. Bouncing back with a sweep — capped by one of the rarest endings in baseball — was exactly the right answer.

Who's been sleeping on the Marlins? They're now 25-29, but games like this matter.

---
facebook.image_caption: Heriberto Hernandez. Pinch-hit. Bases loaded. 0-0 game. Walk-off grand slam. First time in 13 years that's happened in baseball. ⚾

stories.title: Bryan Torres' 11-Year Journey to the Majors — Then He Hit a Homer (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Bryan Torres spent 11 years in the minor leagues.

Friday, he made his MLB debut for the Cardinals.

In the 9th inning, he hit a two-run homer.

First Cardinals player with 2 hits, HR, and walk in a debut since at least 1901.

Worth the wait. ⚾

#BallparkBanter #Cardinals #MLB

facebook.long_form:
Eleven years.

That's how long Bryan Torres spent in the minor leagues before the St. Louis Cardinals finally called him up. From his first minor league appearance in 2015 to his MLB debut on Friday — 11 years of bus rides, Double-A hotels, and the daily hope that today might be the day someone noticed.

He was 28 years old when he got the call.

In his first MLB at-bat, Torres recorded a hit. Then another hit. Then a walk. Then, in the 9th inning of a Cardinals blowout, he drove a 2-run homer 377 feet to right-center field.

That performance — two hits, a walk, and a home run — was the first time any Cardinals player had done that in a debut since at least 1901. Torres also became the first Puerto Rican-born player to homer in his MLB debut since Isan Díaz in 2019.

Baseball is a sport where some people come up at 22 and some people come up at 28. Bryan Torres spent 11 years proving he belonged. On Friday, he proved it in the best way possible.

That's the kind of story that makes baseball worth watching.

---
facebook.image_caption: 11 years in the minors. Then Bryan Torres hit a homer in his MLB debut. Baseball doesn't always follow the script — and that's what makes it great. ⚾

stories.title: Schwarber Pace Check — Still on Historic Track Toward 60+ HRs (Tier 2)

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Memorial Day checkpoint: Kyle Schwarber.

Hit his 20th HR on May 15 — the earliest EVER in MLB history.

Pace: 66-72 home runs by season's end.

Barry Bonds hit his 20th in 2001 on May 19.

No one has been here since Bonds. ⚾

#BallparkBanter #Phillies #MLB

facebook.long_form:
At Memorial Day, baseball takes stock of where the season stands. And when it comes to Kyle Schwarber, what stands out is remarkable.

Schwarber hit his 20th home run of the 2026 season on May 15. Not just impressive — historic. That's the earliest any player in MLB history has hit 20 home runs in a single season. Barry Bonds, in the year he hit 73, didn't get to 20 until May 19. Schwarber beat him by four days.

At his current pace, Schwarber is projecting to 66-72 home runs by season's end. The 60-HR club — where Barry Bonds (73), Mark McGwire (70), and Sammy Sosa (66/65/63) live — is a group nobody has joined in over two decades. Schwarber is knocking on the door.

The Philadelphia Phillies have built around this lineup for exactly this moment, and with Schwarber at his peak, the National League East race has a different dimension than it would without him.

What's the over-under on his final total? Comment below.

---
facebook.image_caption: Kyle Schwarber hit his 20th homer on May 15 — the earliest in MLB history. His pace projects to 66-72 for the season. No one has been here since Barry Bonds. ⚾

stories.title: Memorial Day MLB Schedule — 12 Games, Yankees-Royals on ESPN (Tier 3)

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
Happy Memorial Day. 12 MLB games today. ⚾

Yankees vs. Royals on ESPN at 3:30 PM.

At 3 PM local — every ballpark pauses for the National Moment of Remembrance.

Red poppies on every jersey. 🇺🇸

#BallparkBanter #MLB #Baseball

facebook.long_form:
Happy Memorial Day from Ballpark Banter. Baseball is back today — and the league is honoring the day properly.

All 30 teams are wearing red poppies on their jerseys, with "Lest We Forget" text included. Every ballpark with an active game at 3:00 PM local time will pause for an extended moment of silence as part of the National Moment of Remembrance. It's one of the sport's nicest traditions.

On the field, 12 games are scheduled for Memorial Day Monday. The featured matchup on ESPN at 3:30 PM ET sends the Yankees to Kansas City to face the Royals — Aaron Judge coming off last night's walk-off against the Rays, vs. Bobby Witt Jr. and the Royals at Kauffman Stadium. That's must-see afternoon baseball.

Elsewhere: the Phillies face the Padres (Schwarber watch), the D-Backs host the Giants (Carroll chasing that triples record), and the Cubs open against the Pirates.

Take a moment at 3 PM. Then enjoy some baseball.

---
facebook.image_caption: 12 MLB games today, red poppies on jerseys, and a moment of silence at 3 PM. Memorial Day baseball is a tradition worth honoring. ⚾

stories.title: Memorial Day Standings — Rays, Braves, Brewers Setting the Pace (Tier 3)

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
MLB at Memorial Day 2026:

🏆 AL: Rays lead the East, best record in the league
🏆 NL: Braves lead the East, Brewers winning 15 of last 19

Yankees 4.5 back after Judge's walk-off.

The races are real. ⚾

#BallparkBanter #MLB #Baseball

facebook.long_form:
Memorial Day is baseball's first real checkpoint. A third of the season is in the books. Enough games to start drawing conclusions. Here's where things stand in 2026.

In the American League, the Tampa Bay Rays remain the team to beat — they own the league's best record and are running away with the AL East after consecutive dominant months. The Yankees closed within 4.5 games after Aaron Judge's walk-off Sunday, but Tampa Bay has been relentless all season.

In the National League, the Atlanta Braves are the standard-bearer in the East. And perhaps the most surprising team in all of baseball: the Milwaukee Brewers. They've won 15 of their last 19 games and currently hold the third-best record in the majors. The Brewers have been making franchise history with their pitching staff and lineup depth — this isn't a fluke.

Memorial Day records are a decent predictor: historically, teams with strong Memorial Day records have a solid chance of making the postseason. The Rays, Braves, and Brewers are all building something real.

Who do you think wins it all this year? Let us know below.

---
facebook.image_caption: Memorial Day standings: Rays lead the AL, Braves lead the NL, and the Brewers are winning 15 of their last 19. A third of the season gone. ⚾
